req.title: Reset employee password
req.statement: An employee shall reset their password via an email link that expires after 30 minutes; expired links must be rejected.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers four distinct behavioural zones: (1) happy-path reset with a valid token, (2) rejection of an expired token (410), (3) rejection of a policy-violating password (422), and (4) the boundary instant at which a token transitions from valid to expired (exactly 30 minutes). EP partitions the token space into valid/expired and the password space into policy-compliant/non-compliant. BVA targets the 30-minute expiry edge. STC models the token lifecycle states (issued → used / issued → expired). NEG covers the two documented error pathways. DT cross-cuts token validity against password policy. Together these five tests achieve full requirement coverage per ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4.

tests.name: test_reset_password_with_valid_token_succeeds
preconditions: Employee account exists in the system
A valid, unexpired reset token has been issued within the last 29 minutes
New password satisfies all policy rules
actions: Navigate to /password/reset with the valid token as a query parameter
expected: HTTP 200 (or redirect to success page) is returned
Password is updated in the database
All existing sessions for the employee are invalidated
Confirmation message is displayed on the UI
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import Page

def test_reset_password_with_valid_token_succeeds(
    page: Page,
    valid_reset_token: str,       # <fixture> issues token < 29 min old
    api_client,                   # <fixture> authenticated API helper
    base_url: str,
):
    new_password = "ValidP@ssw0rd!"
    reset_url = f"{base_url}/password/reset?token={valid_reset_token}"

    page.goto(reset_url)
    page.fill("[data-testid='new-password']", new_password)
    page.fill("[data-testid='confirm-password']", new_password)
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-reset']")

    assert page.url.endswith("/password/reset/success") or \
           page.locator("[data-testid='success-banner']").is_visible()

    # Verify session invalidation via API
    sessions = api_client.get("/api/sessions").json()
    assert sessions["active_count"] == 0, "All sessions must be invalidated"

tests.name: test_reset_password_with_expired_token_returns_410
preconditions: Employee account exists in the system
A reset token that was issued more than 30 minutes ago exists (expired)
actions: Navigate to /password/reset with the expired token as a query parameter
Attempt to submit a new password
expected: HTTP 410 Gone is returned by the server
UI displays an 'expired link' error message
Password is NOT changed in the database
trace: R-WEB-102: expired links must be rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import Page

def test_reset_password_with_expired_token_returns_410(
    page: Page,
    expired_reset_token: str,   # <fixture> issues token > 30 min old
    api_client,                 # <fixture> authenticated API helper
    base_url: str,
):
    reset_url = f"{base_url}/password/reset?token={expired_reset_token}"

    response = api_client.post(
        "/api/password/reset",
        json={"token": expired_reset_token, "password": "ValidP@ssw0rd!"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 410, (
        f"Expected 410 for expired token, got {response.status_code}"
    )

    page.goto(reset_url)
    error_locator = page.locator("[data-testid='error-expired-link']")
    assert error_locator.is_visible(), "Expired-link error must be visible"

tests.name: test_reset_password_with_policy_violating_password_returns_422
preconditions: Employee account exists in the system
A valid, unexpired reset token has been issued
New password violates at least one policy rule (e.g. fewer than 8 characters, no special character)
actions: POST to /api/password/reset with the valid token and a non-compliant password
Observe the response status and body
expected: Response body contains a descriptive validation error
Password is NOT changed in the database
tests.executable:
import pytest

@pytest.mark.parametrize("bad_password,reason", [
    ("short",          "too_short"),
    ("nouppercase1!",  "no_uppercase"),
    ("NOLOWER1!",      "no_lowercase"),
    ("NoSpecialChar1", "no_special"),
])
def test_reset_password_with_policy_violating_password_returns_422(
    bad_password: str,
    reason: str,
    valid_reset_token: str,  # <fixture> issues token < 29 min old
    api_client,              # <fixture> authenticated API helper
):
    response = api_client.post(
        "/api/password/reset",
        json={"token": valid_reset_token, "password": bad_password},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 422, (
        f"[{reason}] Expected 422, got {response.status_code}"
    )
    body = response.json()
    assert "errors" in body, "Response must contain validation errors"

tests.name: test_reset_token_at_exact_30_minute_boundary_is_rejected
preconditions: Employee account exists in the system
A reset token whose age is exactly 30 minutes (1800 seconds) is available
actions: POST to /api/password/reset with the boundary-age token and a valid password
expected: HTTP 410 Gone is returned because a token aged exactly 30 minutes is considered expired
trace: R-WEB-102: email link that expires after 30 minutes
R-WEB-102: expired links must be rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

EXPIRY_SECONDS = 1800  # 30 minutes exactly

def test_reset_token_at_exact_30_minute_boundary_is_rejected(
    api_client,             # <fixture> authenticated API helper
    issue_reset_token,      # <helper_fn>(age_seconds) -> token string
):
    boundary_token = issue_reset_token(age_seconds=EXPIRY_SECONDS)

    response = api_client.post(
        "/api/password/reset",
        json={"token": boundary_token, "password": "ValidP@ssw0rd!"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 410, (
        f"Token aged exactly {EXPIRY_SECONDS}s must be rejected with 410, "
        f"got {response.status_code}"
    )

tests.name: test_reset_token_transitions_from_valid_to_used_state
preconditions: Employee account exists in the system
A valid, unexpired reset token has been issued
actions: Submit a valid password reset using the token (token transitions ISSUED -> USED)
Attempt to reuse the same token for a second reset
expected: First submission returns HTTP 200 and password is updated
Second submission returns HTTP 410, confirming single-use token semantics
Token state is USED (cannot be replayed)
trace: R-WEB-102: expired links must be rejected
